verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (kallikrein-related peptidase 2) is a serine protease secreted into the extracellular space and body fluids (blood, seminal plasma, urine). It is synthesized with a signal peptide, undergoes proteolytic maturation in the secretory pathway, and is released as a soluble enzyme. KLK2 has no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no documented stable membrane anchor. While it circulates in blood and can interact with extracellular matrix and other secreted proteins, it does not reach the outer face of the plasma membrane via its own mechanism. It remains in equilibrium with the soluble extracellular pool and would wash away with buffer exchange. Not a surface-accessible drug target.
